name: Computational Complexity: Big O Notation Visualizer
description:
This chart visualizes the growth rates of common algorithmic time complexities, fundamental to Computer Science and Software Engineering.

Key Insights:
  • O(1) & O(log n): Excellent scalability; the execution time remains nearly constant regardless of input size.
  • O(n): Linear growth; typically seen in simple loops through arrays.
  • O(n log n): The complexity of efficient sorting algorithms like Merge Sort or Quick Sort.
  • O(n²) & O(n³): Polynomial growth; performance degrades rapidly as n increases, often involving nested loops.
  • O(2ⁿ): Exponential growth; the complexity doubles with each additional input, making large inputs computationally infeasible.
The chart is restricted to a specific range of n to demonstrate the point at which different algorithms begin to diverge significantly.

plotLayout.title: Growth of Operations vs. Input Size (n)
